content: 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory systems can be restructured to leverage A2A protocols to enable distributed curiosity-driven memory pruning and expansion, where individual agents actively query peer agents for high-uncertainty memory chunks missing from their own knowledge stores instead of only sampling unlabeled data from a centralized corpus. This creates a networked active learning loop where the collective memory of the multi-agent system improves faster than any single agent’s memory can improve in isolation.
